mission.title: vllm: Support OpenAI Responses API interface
mission.description: Add support for the OpenAI Responses API interface in vLLM, keeping compatibility with OpenAI's latest API updates. Requested by 52+ users.

steps.title: Check current vLLM setup
steps.description:
Verify your vLLM version and configuration:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
# or
python -c "import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)"
```
The Responses API support requires a recent version of vLLM.

steps.title: Understand the Responses API
steps.description: OpenAI released a new Responses API that extends the existing completions and chat completions endpoints. vLLM needed to add compatibility with this API to stay in sync with OpenAI's updates. The Responses API provides a unified interface for handling multi-turn conversations with tool use.

steps.title: Upgrade vLLM to a version with Responses API support
steps.description:
Install or upgrade vLLM to get the Responses API support:
```bash
pip install vllm --upgrade
```
The Responses API implementation was added via PR #20504 in the vLLM repository.

steps.title: Start the vLLM server
steps.description:
Launch the vLLM OpenAI-compatible API server:
```bash
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model <your-model-name>
```
The server exposes the Responses API endpoint alongside the existing completions and chat completions endpoints.

steps.title: Verify the Responses API works
steps.description:
Test the Responses API endpoint:
```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/responses \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "<your-model>", "input": "Explain quantum computing in one sentence."}'
```
You should receive a response in the OpenAI Responses API format.

resolution.summary: vLLM added support for the OpenAI Responses API interface, enabling compatibility with OpenAI's latest API format. This allows users to use the Responses API endpoint alongside existing completions and chat completions endpoints when serving models with vLLM.
codeSnippets:
# Start vLLM with Responses API support
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model <your-model>

# Test using Llama Stack with vLLM backend
podman run --rm -it \
  --network=host \
  --env VLLM_URL="http://localhost:8000" \
  --env INFERENCE_MODEL="RedHatAI/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-FP8" \
  docker.io/llamastack/distribution-remote-vllm

prerequisites.description: Python 3.8+, pip, a CUDA-capable GPU, and a working vLLM installation (pip install vllm).
